other_observers: Dennis Myrick; Bill Nichols (neighbor) first saw the Oriole on Sunday, February 19. He mentioned this me when I told him about seeing the Oriole.
description: Black head and back with white wing markings. Orange breast with black chin. Orange supercilium with black line through eye to black back; has blackish cheek patch under eye. Rump is orange, tail dark feathers.   It is the black coloration on his cheek that says he has some Baltimore Oriole in his past breeding. And the white on his wings is not as strong as on a Bullock's.  Size 9"Behaviors: He has been coming to our Woodpecker Bar and Bell feeders for over a month. He eats from the feeder then sometimes sits in the tree in our or our neighbor's yard for up to an hour before flying off. He has been seen at any time between 7:00 AM and 1 PM. I have never seen him in the afternoon.Call: None heardPlumage: Male Breeding
